product_overview: CommUnity is a digital platform for managing community social activities end-to-end, including user registration, organizer verification, event coordination, volunteer participation, reporting, and certificate issuance. The MVP focuses on centralizing operational workflows that are currently handled across disconnected tools and manual processes.
core_goals: Reduce manual administration of social activities by at least 50%
Centralize community activity, participant, and reporting data in one platform
Support volunteer onboarding, event participation, and account/profile management
Provide a secure authentication flow with role-based access control
Enable a scalable foundation for attendance, reporting, analytics, and digital certificate workflows

features.name: Health Check
features.description: Provides a lightweight endpoint to verify that the CommUnity API service is running and reachable.
user_flows: GET /api/v1/health -> Receive 200 with success=true and a service message
GET /api/v1/health during temporary service issues -> Receive non-200 response or error payload indicating the API is unavailable

features.name: User Registration
features.description: Allows new volunteer users to create an account and receive an authentication token for immediate access.
user_flows: POST /api/v1/auth/register with valid full_name, username, email, password, and password_confirmation -> Receive 201 with user data and Sanctum token -> Use returned token for authenticated requests
POST /api/v1/auth/register with missing or invalid fields -> Receive 422 validation error with field-level messages

features.name: User Login
features.description: Authenticates existing users and returns a token for accessing protected features.
user_flows: POST /api/v1/auth/login with valid username and password -> Receive 200 with user data and Sanctum token -> Use token to access protected endpoints
POST /api/v1/auth/login with incorrect credentials -> Receive 422 invalid credentials error and no token

features.name: User Logout
features.description: Ends the current authenticated session by revoking the access token.
user_flows: POST /api/v1/auth/logout with a valid bearer token -> Receive 200 confirming logout -> Subsequent GET /api/v1/profile with the same token -> Receive 401 unauthenticated
POST /api/v1/auth/logout without a token or with an expired token -> Receive 401 unauthenticated

features.name: Forgot Password
features.description: Starts the password reset process by generating a reset token for a registered email address.
user_flows: POST /api/v1/auth/forgot-password with a registered email -> Receive 200 with reset_token and message -> Use the token in the reset flow
POST /api/v1/auth/forgot-password with an invalid or unregistered email format -> Receive 422 validation error or failed request response

features.name: Reset Password
features.description: Resets a user password using email and reset token so the user can regain access to the account.
user_flows: POST /api/v1/auth/reset-password with valid email, token, password, and password_confirmation -> Receive 200 confirming password reset -> POST /api/v1/auth/login with new password -> Receive 200 with a new token
POST /api/v1/auth/reset-password with an invalid or expired token -> Receive 422 invalid or expired token error

features.name: Profile Management
features.description: Lets authenticated users view and update their profile details, including uploading a profile photo.
user_flows: POST /api/v1/auth/login with valid credentials -> Receive 200 with token -> GET /api/v1/profile with bearer token -> Receive 200 with current user profile -> PATCH /api/v1/profile with updated full_name, username, or email -> Receive 200 with updated profile
GET /api/v1/profile without a bearer token -> Receive 401 unauthenticated -> PATCH /api/v1/profile with invalid or duplicate data -> Receive 422 validation error
POST /api/v1/profile/photo with bearer token and a valid image file -> Receive 200 with updated UserResource including photo data
POST /api/v1/profile/photo with bearer token and an invalid file type or missing file -> Receive 422 validation error
